description: Generates personalized interview questions from Claude Code session history and project code

evals.name: Full workflow - Chinese prompt
evals.prompt: 我刚用 Claude Code 开发完一个视频脚本生成 Agent，用了 LangChain.js + BullMQ + NestJS，下周要面试字节跳动的高级前端工程师职位，帮我从我的 claude 记录里准备面试题，项目在 ~/projects/video-script-agent
evals.expected_output: The assistant should: (1) confirm the project path and target role, (2) use Bash tool to run scripts/run.sh ~/projects/video-script-agent to extract session decisions and code summary, (3) use Read tool to read extracted files and references, (4) build a knowledge graph cross-referencing both documents with confidence ratings (🟢🟡🔴), (5) use Write tool to generate project_knowledge_graph.md, (6) generate 20-30 interview questions anchored to actual architecture decisions using Write tool to interview_questions.md, (7) produce STAR story cards using Write tool to story_cards.md. All steps should be automated without requiring user to manually copy-paste.
validation_criteria: project_knowledge_graph.md contains ≥5 architecture decisions
interview_questions.md contains questions referencing specific module names
story_cards.md contains 5 STAR-format story cards in first-person Chinese
All files generated using Write tool (no manual copy-paste)

evals.name: Missing project path - should ask
evals.prompt: I've been building an internal React dashboard tool with Claude Code for the past 2 months — it uses React + Zustand + React Query on the frontend and a simple Express API. I have a fullstack engineer interview at a Series B startup next Friday. Can you help me prepare?
evals.expected_output: The assistant should ask for the project directory path (since it wasn't provided) using AskUserQuestion tool. After receiving the path, it should proceed with the full workflow: run extraction scripts, build knowledge graph, generate interview questions and story cards. The knowledge graph should identify key decisions made (Zustand vs Redux, React Query for server state, API design choices). Interview questions should include both system design and behavioral questions. Story cards should be in English, first-person.
validation_criteria: Uses AskUserQuestion to request project path
After receiving path, proceeds with full automated workflow

evals.name: Monorepo project
evals.prompt: 帮我从我的 Claude Code 开发记录里整理面试题，我的项目是个 monorepo，用了 Turborepo + Next.js 14 + Prisma + tRPC，还有一些 Agent 工具，项目目录是 /Users/me/work/saas-platform
evals.expected_output: The assistant should recognize this as a monorepo with multiple concerns and run session-extractor to find decisions across all workspaces. Knowledge graph should map decisions per package (web app, API, agent tools). Interview questions should cover: monorepo build decisions (Turborepo cache strategy), tRPC vs REST tradeoffs, Prisma schema design, Next.js app router adoption, and any Agent tool architecture decisions. Should note any 'tension points' where session logs indicate considered alternatives but chose differently.
validation_criteria: Knowledge graph includes decisions from multiple packages
Interview questions reference specific monorepo decisions

evals.name: No session data - fallback to code analysis
evals.prompt: 帮我准备面试，项目目录是 ~/projects/new-project（一个刚创建的项目，还没怎么用 Claude Code 开发）
evals.expected_output: The assistant should run the extraction scripts. When session-extractor returns empty or minimal extracted_decisions.md, it should gracefully fall back to generating interview questions based primarily on code_summary.md. The assistant should inform the user that session data is sparse and the knowledge graph will rely mainly on code analysis. Interview questions should still reference specific module names found in the code.
validation_criteria: Handles empty extracted_decisions.md gracefully
Informs user about sparse session data
Generates interview questions from code analysis
Confidence ratings adjusted accordingly (mostly 🟡/🔴)

evals.name: No git repository
evals.prompt: 准备面试，项目目录是 ~/projects/experiment-demo，这是一个实验性项目，还没有初始化 git
evals.expected_output: The assistant should run extraction scripts successfully. When code_analyzer.sh reports no git history, it should skip the git history section in code_summary.md but still extract directory structure and dependencies. The knowledge graph should be built from available information (directory structure + session data).
validation_criteria: code_summary.md generated without git history section
Knowledge graph built successfully without git data
No errors or crashes

evals.name: Very large project - should handle gracefully
evals.prompt: 帮我准备面试，项目在 ~/projects/huge-enterprise-app，这个项目有上千个文件
evals.expected_output: The assistant should run extraction with appropriate limits. If code_analyzer.sh takes too long or generates >100KB output, it should suggest using filters (--days, --max-files) or process only the most relevant parts. The assistant should warn about large context size before proceeding with LLM steps.
validation_criteria: Warns about large project size
Suggests optimization options
Does not crash or hang

evals.name: English prompt - should respond in English
evals.prompt: I have an interview next week for a senior frontend position. I've been working on a Vue 3 + TypeScript + Pinia project using Claude Code. Can you help me prepare interview questions based on my project? The project is at ~/projects/vue-dashboard
evals.expected_output: The assistant should respond in English throughout the workflow. The knowledge graph and interview questions should be in English. STAR story cards should be in first-person English. The skill should detect the user's language preference from the prompt and match it.
validation_criteria: All output files in English
Story cards in first-person English
Language matches user prompt

evals.name: Invalid project path - error handling
evals.expected_output: The assistant should check if the project directory exists before running scripts. If the path is invalid, it should inform the user clearly and ask for the correct path. It should not crash or produce empty files silently.
validation_criteria: Checks directory existence before running scripts
Provides clear error message
Asks for correct path
